sections.paragraph: 1Sa 15:9, refuse, vile (used of cattle); LXX. ἡτιμωμένον, Vulg. vile, i.q. נִבְזֶה. There is no similar instance of a word so irregularly and monstrously formed, and it seems to have arisen from a blending of two, מִבְזָה (abstr. for concr.) and נִבְזֶה which appears like a gloss. See Lehrg. 462, 63.
